message:
fix(cx): early-return attenuate_from_budget without macaroon; DRY child task construction

Two related improvements to the capability context (Cx) subsystem:

1. Cx::attenuate_from_budget() (cx.rs) — Added an early-return guard
   that checks for the presence of a macaroon before proceeding to budget
   inspection.  Previously, a Cx without a macaroon would fall through to
   the budget.deadline.map_or_else branch and return Some(self.clone()),
   which is misleading: attenuating a context that has no macaroon to
   attenuate should return None, not a clone.  Added three tests:
   - Returns None when no macaroon is attached
   - Preserves the token (zero new caveats) when budget has no deadline
   - Adds exactly one deadline caveat when budget has a deadline

2. Scope::build_child_task_cx() (scope.rs) — Extracted a shared helper
   that constructs the child task's capability context from the parent Cx
   and RuntimeState.  The identical 18-line block was duplicated verbatim
   in spawn() and spawn_blocking().  The extracted method also wires up
   the timer_driver and logical_clock handles (via the new
   new_with_drivers constructor and with_logical_clock builder), ensuring
   spawned child tasks inherit the runtime's timer infrastructure.
   Added two tests verifying that both spawn() and spawn_blocking()
   produce child contexts with has_timer() == true when the runtime has
   a timer driver configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
